what_it_does: Exports the WireRequest React component, a floating operator door that appears only when a ui:// element is indicated in annotate mode. It lets the operator describe a change, mint a build-intent via mintBuildIntent, review the persisted intent's X16 blast radius through BlastRadiusReach, and approve the intent via api.resolve; it stays inert/plan-mode by default and does not itself dispatch edits.
observations: Renders only when indicated exists and clickMode(indicated) === 'annotate', matching AnnotateBar's gate.
Uses useApp for indicated, clickMode, mintBuildIntent, resolveUiTarget, and the live inbox.
Resolves the full persisted minted item from inbox.live_escalations by id, falling back to the flat mint return until the inbox catches up.
Reuses BlastRadiusReach for reach visualization; shows a deny-all message when payload.blast_radius is absent.
Approval calls api.resolve(minted.id, 'approve') and sets an approved flag; dispatch is left to a backend watcher.
Includes a static inert/plan-mode notice because the armed state is not exposed to the FE.
Supports Cmd/Ctrl+Enter to submit the change description.
Sets data-ui-ref='ui://canvas/wire-request' on the outer container so the registry can resolve the door itself.
standouts: Closes the repo-known UI gap: it is the FE caller for POST /api/intent-at (mintBuildIntent).
Reuses BlastRadiusReach instead of reimplementing blast-radius visualization.
Honest safe-by-default design: no live armed indicator, only static copy stating plan-mode default.
conventions: Follows React functional-component conventions with useState hooks.
Imports and uses design-kit primitives (Badge) and existing components (BlastRadiusReach).
Uses keyboard shortcut Cmd/Ctrl+Enter for primary action.
Breaks strict typing by using any for fallback and inbox item shapes.
concerns: mintedFallback is typed as any and may have a different shape from the full persisted inbox item, so BlastRadiusReach could receive an inconsistent d object.
No visible handling if mintBuildIntent returns an error object; it relies on the caller surfacing a notice.
The deny-all fallback is shown whenever payload.blast_radius is missing, even if the flat return might carry scope information.
approve sets setErr but does not clear a previous approved state if called again after a transient failure.
notes: A self-build wire door that mints build-intents at pointed UI elements and makes the operator see blast radius before approving, while honestly staying in plan-mode by default.
questions: What exact shape does mintBuildIntent return, and what fields does the full inbox item contain?
How does clickMode decide an element is in 'annotate' mode versus operate mode?
What does BlastRadiusReach expect in its d prop beyond payload.blast_radius?
How is the door positioned visually over the canvas, since CSS is not shown here?
What response shape does api.resolve return, and what errors can it surface?
Is inbox.live_escalations guaranteed to contain the minted item by the time mintBuildIntent resolves?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is explicitly the FE caller for the build-intent wire and implements the described point-ask-see-approve flow.
apparent_themes: operator consent through visible blast radius
reflects-never-owns (reads persisted intent from inbox rather than caching)
intention_signals: To give operators a transparent, consent-first way to request changes to UI elements by composing plans, seeing computed reach, and recording approval, while leaving actual dispatch gated by backend posture and a watcher.
novelty: It is a rare self-modification interface door that mints intents from a pointed UI address and immediately surfaces an existing blast-radius component for consent, deliberately avoiding a live armed-state indicator.
contribution: A focused React surface that connects indicated UI elements to the build-intent lifecycle (mint + approve), reuses the existing blast-radius visualization, and encodes the safe-by-default plan-mode contract in its static copy and flow.
summary_for_embedding: WireRequest is a React component that renders a conditional floating door for minting and approving build-intents against indicated ui:// canvas elements, reusing BlastRadiusReach to show blast radius before approval.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the missing frontend caller for the decision-to-implementation wire with an honest, consent-first, plan-mode interface.

symbols.does: Renders the floating wire-request door for pointed UI elements.
symbols.description: WireRequest is the exported React component that conditionally renders when a ui:// element is in annotate mode. It orchestrates describing a change, minting a build-intent, resolving the persisted inbox item, showing its blast radius, and approving it.

symbols.does: Mints a build-intent from the operator's description.
symbols.description: The request handler trims the textarea value, calls mintBuildIntent, and on success stores the returned id and fallback summary so the door can resolve the full persisted item and display its reach.

symbols.does: Records an approve verdict for the minted build-intent.
symbols.description: The approve handler calls api.resolve with the minted intent id and 'approve', sets an approved flag on success, and surfaces any error; it does not itself dispatch the change.

symbols.does: Resets the door's minted and approval state.
symbols.description: The dismiss handler clears mintedId, mintedFallback, approved, and err, returning the door to the ask state while leaving the intent in the Inbox builds lane.
